LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS JOHN CHIANG, IN HIS CAPACITY AS CALIFORNIA STATE CONTROLLER, RANDALL A. DEEMS, IN CAPACITY AS ACTING DIRECTOR, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HOUSING AND COMMUNITY AND REBECCA MATT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. LEAVE TO AMEND IS DENIED BECAUSE BASED ON THESE FACTS, NO AMENDMENT TO THE APPLICATION CAN CURE THIS FLAW. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION AS THE COMPLAINT FAILS TO STATE ANY UNDERLYING CLAIM WHICH WOULD ENTITLE PLAINTIFFS TO INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES AND ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; SHERYL L. SAWYER CSR# 5976. (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-05-2015 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-21-2014 Continued To Jan-21-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-01-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Wayne K. Snodgrass, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.4675, for defendants and moving party;
Steven L. Hammond, Esq. of Hammond Law Group, Tel. 415.955.1915, for plaintiffs and opposing party;
Christiana Tiedemann, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Tel. 510.622.2218, for defendants Chiang, etc.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, EDWIN LEE and TRENT RHORER'S AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE Portions Of The COMPLAINT is granted. The allegations in paragraphs 66-78 provide no basis for relief in a taxpayer lawsuit. 25 CCR Section 7960 contains no legal requirement that an application for funding include the information allegedly absent from the City's application, and Plaintiffs have pointed to no other law mandating the inclusion of this information. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Sheryl L. Sawyer CSR# 5976.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-01-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Christiana Tiedemann, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Tel. 510.622.2218, for defendants and moving party;
Steven L. Hammond, Esq. of Hammond Law Group, Tel. 415.955.1915, for plaintiffs and opposing party;
Wayne K. Snodgrass, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.4675, for defendant CCSF, etc.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS JOHN CHIANG, IN HIS CAPACITY AS CALIFORNIA STATE CONTROLLER, RANDALL A. DEEMS, IN CAPACITY AS ACTING DIRECTOR, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HOUSING AND COMMUNITY and REBECCA MATT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The Demurrer is sustained as to the first cause of action. None of the alleged defects in the funding application provides a basis for a taxpayer claim under CCP 526a, as none of the alleged defects is a violation of a legal obligation. Leave to amend is denied because based on these facts, no amendment to the application can cure this flaw. The Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the second cause of action as the Complaint fails to state any underlying claim which would entitle Plaintiffs to injunctive relief. The Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the third cause of action. First, based on the Court's determination that the alleged application defects are not violations of law but rather discretionary decisions made by public officials, the Complaint fails to state a claim because such decisions are not subject to interference by the Court. Second, as to the alleged controversy regarding the disbursement of funds, Plaintiffs' request for leave to amend is granted to permit Plaintiffs to clarify the nature of the claim. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Sheryl L. Sawyer CSR# 5976.
